parameters.text: =✅ Route expenses with Gmail and Google Drive end to end — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### New Task Form Submission

Serves an n8n-hosted form (Title, Assignee, Duedate). Each submission becomes one execution.

Next: Stamp & Shape Task

### Stamp & Shape Task

Shapes the item: writes `taskId`, `receivedAt`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Payload Valid?

### Payload Valid?

Two-way gate on `={{ $json.taskId }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Route by Work Type · ②→ Record Rejected Input

### Record Rejected Input

Appends a new row to 'Task Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Route by Work Type

Routes each item to one of 3 lanes: auto-approve, needs-review + fallback.

Ports: ①→ Process 'auto-approve' → Google Sheets · ②→ Fast-Track 'needs-review' + Alert · ③→ Hold Unknown Types (DLQ)

### Process 'auto-approve' → Google Sheets

Upserts a row into 'Task Tracker', matched on `taskId` — re-running with the same key updates the row instead of duplicating it.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Fast-Track 'needs-review' + Alert

Sends an email via Gmail — subject is the first line of the message body, recipient comes from the item's `email` field.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Hold Unknown Types (DLQ)

Appends a new row to 'Task Tracker' — a human-readable audit trail that doesn't require opening n8n to inspect.

Ports: ①→ Merge Routed Work · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Merge Routed Work

Rejoins 3 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Guard Non-Empty title

### Guard Non-Empty title

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Tag Pipeline Metadata

### Tag Pipeline Metadata

Shapes the item: writes `pipeline`, `version`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Send Summary to Slack

### Send Summary to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#projects`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

Posts a message to Slack (`#projects`) — a low-latency way for the team to see automation activity without checking a dashboard.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Route expenses with Gmail and Google Drive end to end

**Pattern:** Approval Workflow · **Level:** Core · **Trigger:** Form

## What it does
Route expenses with Gmail and Google Drive end to end. It starts from a hosted form submission.

## Why it exists
Manual onboarding is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
New Task Form Submission → Stamp & Shape Task → Payload Valid? → Route by Work Type → Process 'auto-approve' → Google Sheets → Merge Routed Work → Guard Non-Empty title → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to approval workflow without manual effort
- Anyone automating onboarding in software development
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Removes copy-paste errors
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Responds in seconds

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2; Google OAuth2 / service account; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

Unrecognized work types are parked in a dead-letter lane instead of erroring the whole run or silently dropping the item — new event kinds will show up eventually, and this is where you'd notice one without losing it. One kind is deliberately routed through a sub-workflow to show how heavier per-type logic can be extracted without touching this router at all.

## Practice exercises

Once it's wired up with real credentials, try:

1. Send a work-type value that matches no switch rule and confirm it lands in the dead-letter branch.
2. Add a new work type and its own handling branch.
3. Replace the direct-write branch with a sub-workflow call, matching the pattern already used for the first kind.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your onboarding process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
